name: Addington v. Texas
question: Is the proper standard of proof for a civil case of involuntary commitment to a mental health facility whether the evidence presented to the jury was “clear, unequivocal, and convincing”?
description: A case in which the Court held that the standard of proof required in a civil case involving involuntary commitment to a mental health facility must be higher than a preponderance of the evidence, but that the “beyond a reasonable doubt” standard was not constitutionally required.
